column1: Trea Turner leads the charge for Beat The Streak players tonight, bringing a steady bat to the top of Philly’s order at home with a strong 71.9% hit probability and a clear platoon advantage against MacKenzie Gore. Right behind him, Jacob Wilson slots into the two-hole for Toronto and boasts a nearly identical hit profile, facing Eric Lauer in a slightly favorable park—expect Wilson’s reliable approach to keep the streak dreams alive. Jonathan Aranda and Joey Loperfido round out the slate with plus matchups and solid contact rates, but Turner’s top-of-order role and matchup make him the clear-cut pick to anchor any streak.
